root_text.sn45.20:1.3: Katamaṁ nu kho, āvuso, brahmacariyaṁ, katamo brahmacārī, katamaṁ brahmacariyapariyosānan”ti?
root_text.sn45.20:1.5: Bhaddako kho te, āvuso bhadda, ummaṅgo, bhaddakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ, kalyāṇī paripucchā.
root_text.sn45.20:1.8: Katamaṁ nu kho, āvuso, brahmacariyaṁ, katamo brahmacārī, katamaṁ brahmacariyapariyosānan’”ti?
root_text.sn45.20:1.10: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo brahmacariyaṁ, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn45.20:1.12: Yo kho, āvuso, iminā ariyena aṭṭhaṅgikena maggena samannāgato—
root_text.sn45.20:1.14: Yo kho, āvuso, rāgakkhayo dosakkhayo mohakkhayo—

translation_text.sn45.20:1.2: “Reverend, they speak of this thing called the ‘spiritual path’.
translation_text.sn45.20:1.3: What is the spiritual path? Who is someone on the spiritual path? And what is the culmination of the spiritual path?”
translation_text.sn45.20:1.5: Your approach and articulation are excellent, and it’s a good question. …
translation_text.sn45.20:1.10: The spiritual path is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn45.20:1.11: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn45.20:1.12: Someone who possesses this noble eightfold path
translation_text.sn45.20:1.13: is called someone on the spiritual path.
translation_text.sn45.20:1.14: The ending of greed, hate, and delusion:
translation_text.sn45.20:1.15: this is the culmination of the spiritual path.”
